verses.text: David prays for preservation, trusting wholly in God, and owns that his goodness adds nothing to the Most High. He brings empty hands. We do not enrich God by our service; all our good is owed to Him.

David prays for preservation, trusting wholly in God, and owns that his goodness adds nothing to the Most High. He brings empty hands. We do not enrich God by our service; all our good is owed to Him.

The saints are his chief delight, while those who chase after other gods only multiply their sorrows. He chooses godly company. The path of idols leads to grief; the fellowship of the holy is true joy.

The saints are his chief delight, while those who chase after other gods only multiply their sorrows. He chooses godly company. The path of idols leads to grief; the fellowship of the holy is true joy.

'The LORD is the portion of mine inheritance,' he says; the boundary lines have fallen for him in pleasant places. God Himself is his treasure. To have the Lord as our portion is wealth no earthly loss can touch.

'The LORD is the portion of mine inheritance,' he says; the boundary lines have fallen for him in pleasant places. God Himself is his treasure. To have the Lord as our portion is wealth no earthly loss can touch.

He blesses the LORD for counsel and has 'set the LORD always before me,' so he shall not be moved. A God-centred mind brings steadiness. Keeping the Lord constantly in view holds the heart firm in every trial.

He blesses the LORD for counsel and has 'set the LORD always before me,' so he shall not be moved. A God-centred mind brings steadiness. Keeping the Lord constantly in view holds the heart firm in every trial.

His heart is glad and his flesh rests in hope, for God 'wilt not leave my soul in hell,' but shows the path of life and fulness of joy at His right hand. This is the hope of resurrection. Fulfilled in the risen Christ, it is the sure joy of all who are His.
